scenarios.notes: Reply: mentions skills/agents/chat capabilities. Events: user_message_received emitted, no permission_denied. Fail: artifact direct_llm not present (chat_turn_completed_inline path, no skill_run_spawned for direct_llm).

Reply: explains idempotency factually and comprehensibly. Events: no permission_denied. Fail: artifact direct_llm not present — router used web__search instead of spawning direct_llm skill. routing_decided fired for web__search, not direct_llm.

Reply: lists many skill names (direct_llm, eval, word_stats_demo, etc.) — rubric met. Fail: routing_decided NOT emitted (must_emit fails); artifact direct_llm not present (inline response, no skill_run).

Reply: reports '44文字で、1行で、推定トークン数は1' — character count satisfies rubric. Events: routing_decided ✓, skill_run_spawned ✓, skill_run_completed emitted with status='finished' (rubric specifies status=success — string mismatch). Artifact word_stats_demo present (skill_runs file exists). Inconclusive: skill completed successfully but status literal 'finished' vs rubric 'success'.

Reply: LLM asked for clarification ('どのようなテーマや雰囲気の詩をご希望ですか？') instead of writing a poem — rubric requires poem with at least two lines. Events: chat_turn_completed_inline emitted (satisfies must_emit_any). Artifact direct_llm not present.

Turn 1: explains list comprehension. Turn 2: provides '[x*2 for x in range(1, 6)]' code example — rubric met (Python code example, coherent with topic). Events: no permission_denied. Artifact direct_llm present (skill_run for direct_llm completed on turn 2).
Reply: '画像生成は現在サポートされていません' — politely declines, does not pretend to generate — reply rubric met. Events: no permission_denied. Fail: artifact direct_llm not present (inline response, no skill_run).

new_findings.title: direct_llm artifact absent for inline-routed scenarios
new_findings.description: S1, S3, S5, S7 all received inline responses (chat_turn_completed_inline) with no skill_run_spawned for direct_llm. The rubric requires {skill: direct_llm, present: true} but these scenarios never invoke the direct_llm skill — the router answers inline. This is the dominant R cause (4/5 R scenarios). Pattern consistent with B44 R trajectory.

new_findings.title: S2 routes to web__search instead of direct_llm for factual query
new_findings.description: Factual question '「冪等」とはどういう意味ですか?' triggered web__search (routing_decided for web__search) rather than direct_llm skill invocation. Reply quality was good (answered correctly from web results) but artifact check fails. Regression vs B43/B42 where this may have passed.

new_findings.title: S4 skill_run_completed status field is 'finished' not 'success'
new_findings.description: word_stats_demo completed successfully but emitted skill_run_completed with status='finished'. Rubric checks {status: success}. If 'finished' is the canonical OS status for success, the rubric is wrong; if 'success' is expected, this is an OS status naming regression. Inconclusive verdict assigned pending investigation.

new_findings.title: S5 poem scenario: LLM asks for clarification instead of producing poem
new_findings.description: Short poem request '短い詩を書いてください' resulted in clarifying question about theme/mood rather than a poem. This is a clear behavioral regression — inline router LLM chose to ask rather than act. Pattern may indicate router system prompt over-caution or ambiguity handling shift.
